It belongs to the family Apocynaceae, which is a large and diverse group of plants commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. The genus Schubertia is part of the subfamily Asclepiadoideae, which is a subfamily of Apocynaceae. This subfamily is characterized by its milkweed-like characteristics and is primarily composed of tropical and subtropical plants. The classification of Schubertia longiflora reflects its unique adaptations and evolutionary history within the Apocynaceae family.
